corrected_fields.summary: This Federal Reserve FAQ page explains the mechanics of how the FOMC collects and publishes economic projections from policymakers. Projections are collected four times yearly (March, June, September, December) and cover real GDP growth, unemployment rate, PCE inflation, and core PCE inflation. The page defines how ranges and central tendencies are calculated but does not provide actual projection figures.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: Understanding FOMC projection methodology is foundational for interpreting Fed communications, but this FAQ contains no specific forecasts, data points, or current macro catalysts. It is an educational page about Fed process, not a source of market-relevant information.
